headline: Separating Planning and Execution in AI-Assisted Development
lede: Modern coding requires a shift in how we command language models. By decoupling the architecture of intent from the mechanical act of synthesis, we regain control over the development cycle.

headline: Exploring the vast world of Japanese woodblock prints
lede: A colorful, meticulously indexed archive brings centuries of Japanese artistry to the browser. It is a whimsical digital gallery where every search yields a new visual discovery.

headline: Running Llama 3.1 on consumer hardware by bypassing the CPU
lede: By leveraging NVMe-to-GPU streaming and a custom three-tier caching system, this build pushes the boundaries of localized transformer inference. It is a masterclass in squeezing massive model performance from a single RTX 3090.

headline: How a rogue botnet accidentally crippled the I2P network
lede: Infrastructure is rarely destroyed by design; often, it is the unintended consequences of automated chaos. This breakdown details the day a botnet unintentionally routed the I2P network into total failure.

headline: The immigrant entrepreneurs who built the American toy industry
lede: The evolution of the playroom is inextricably linked to the ambition of early twentieth-century manufacturers. Behind every childhood classic lies a complex history of market competition and innovation.
pullquote: The toy box is a blueprint for the aspirations of a changing American economy.

headline: Doubling bloom filter accuracy with two bits
lede: Probabilistic data structures often require a trade-off between space and error rates. By assigning two bits rather than one, we refine the filter to perform with significantly higher precision.

headline: Inside the architecture of LLM-native silicon chips
lede: Taalas is re-engineering the semiconductor to move beyond general-purpose computing. These chips are designed specifically to print large language model weights directly onto the fabric of the hardware.

headline: Testing the limits of historical English intelligibility
lede: Language is a moving target that drifts further away with every passing generation. We measure the chronological barrier where syntax and vocabulary become unrecognizable to the modern ear.

headline: Evidence of the bouba-kiki effect in naive baby chicks
lede: The tendency to map abstract shapes to sounds appears to be innate rather than learned. Recent empirical study of newly hatched chicks suggests that cross-modal sensory associations may be fundamental to cognitive development.

headline: Reviving multiplayer games long after the servers shut down
lede: The end of official support usually marks the final death of a digital experience. Projects like Gamedate seek to memorialize and restore the abandoned lobbies of a bygone gaming era.
